item.title: US regulators unveil plans to cut Wall Street capital requirements by 4.8%
item.description: Measures for banks would weaken one of the main regulatory guardrails against a financial crisis

item.title: Lloyd’s insurers enjoy bumper profits for third year running
item.description: Chief executive says market welcomes ‘all risks that can be legally insured’

item.title: EQT-backed vet group IVC Evidensia readies IPO
item.description: Private equity-owned company in early-stage talks for London or European stock market listing

item.title: Stocks and bonds hammered as investors price in ‘protracted energy shock’
item.description: Markets in Europe and the US reel after Iranian strikes on Qatari natural gas complex

item.title: Spain poses obstacle to defence merger backed by Dan Loeb’s Third Point
item.description: Aspiring national champion Indra’s mooted takeover of EM&E dogged by questions over role of Escribano brothers

item.title: ‘Armageddon scenario’ for gas markets as Qatar hit by missiles
item.description: Traders and analysts warn of lasting disruption after damage to facility that supplies a fifth of the world’s LNG

item.title: ‘Demand destruction has begun’
item.description: Pray for Asian naphtha consumers

item.title: The ‘great sucking sound’ of AI brain drain
item.description: An explosion in industry salaries has resulted in top talent leaving the public sector

item.title: Bank of England warns of inflation risks as it holds rates at 3.75%
item.description: Assessment from MPC adds to sell-off in gilts and prompts traders to increase bets on higher borrowing costs

item.title: Pandemic brought NHS ‘close to collapse’, says UK Covid inquiry
item.description: Report also says ‘Stay Home, Protect the NHS’ message stopped people receiving treatment for life-threatening conditions

item.title: BofA apologises for bet against European private credit
item.description: US bank says recommendation has been withdrawn ‘based on a review of new information’

item.title: Uber strikes $1.25bn deal with Rivian for robotaxi fleet
item.description: Ride-hailing app to buy as many as 50,000 autonomous vehicles and invest an initial $300mn in California EV company

item.title: Net zero expectations slip to 2070 for some energy executives
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Fervo Energy secures $421mn for next-generation geothermal project

item.title: Macron and Merz make last-ditch push to rescue joint fighter-jet project
item.description: French and German leaders pressure Dassault Aviation and Airbus to resolve differences and save FCAS

item.title: The dynamic globalisation machine will overcome the Iran shock
item.description: Short-term crisis management in shipping should not be mistaken for fatal weakness in trade

item.title: Deloitte tax boss put forward as only candidate in UK leadership vote
item.description: Darren Graves will become Big Four firm’s new chief if approved by colleagues

item.title: How (not) to help with high energy prices
item.description: Heed the lessons from 2022

item.title: FirstFT: Energy prices surge after Iran strikes Qatari LNG site
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: The Federal Reserve holds rates as energy prices rise and Delcy Rodríguez overhauls her cabinet

item.title: Chair of India’s largest private bank quits over ‘ethical differences’
item.description: Central bank calms market saying ‘there are no material concerns’ about the health of HDFC Bank

item.title: Submit your questions to Katie Martin: are markets heading for a shock?
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with the FT’s markets columnist today at 1pm GMT

item.title: A test for chip testing and SoftBank’s stumble
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Middle East war gatecrashes EU leaders’ economy summit
item.description: Also in this newsletter: DHL Express Europe looks on the bright side

item.title: Iran inflicts ‘extensive damage’ on site of world’s largest LNG facility in Qatar
item.description: Tehran vowed to retaliate against Gulf energy plants after its South Pars gasfield was hit

item.title: Kirkland & Ellis, the gilded age
item.description: The firm becomes first to break $10bn in annual revenues while equity partners take home $11.1mn

item.title: BrewDog’s new owner seeks female drinkers to revive brewer
item.description: Irwin Simon said he ‘would love’ to see more women drinkers and dismissed health concerns over moderate alcohol consumption

item.title: Spain is a role model in weathering Iranian oil shocks
item.description: The country’s speedy rollout of renewables has put a lid on electricity bills

item.title: Why bank cards may be heading the way of the cheque book
item.description: The £100 cap on contactless card spending is lifted today — but digital wallets matter more for future growth

item.title: Economists have caught the AI bug
item.description: It’s expanding the scope of research but challenging the profession’s process of weeding out mistakes

item.title: Calls for probe after ex-Army chief pressed minister to buy drones
item.description: Sir Patrick Sanders, an adviser to German start-up Stark, complained that the UK had no orders with the company

item.title: Trump sails into battle with Lloyd’s of London
item.description: The market’s historic strength in marine war insurance is being challenged by the US
